sections.paragraph:
ϰαταντάω [κατά, ἀντάω ‘meet’]

—1. of coming to a destination

—a. of pers. reaching a place in the course of travel come (to), arrive (at) Ac 16:1; 18:24; 25:13; 28:13; reach, in the course of encountering traveling hazards 27:12.

—b. by ext., of someth. inanimate: τὰ τέλη τῶν αἰώνων ϰατήντηϰεν the ends of the world have come/converged 1 Cor 10:11; the word of God 14:36.

—2. fig. ext. of 1, in ref. to a condition or circumstance attain to Ac 26:7; Eph 4:13; Phil 3:11.
